text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little town, there lived a girl named Lily. Lily loved to sew beautiful quilts. She spent hours folding fabrics and stitching together patterns. One day, she decided to create a quilt that would describe a story she loved. She chose two different patterns, one for each part of the tale. Then, she quilted a pattern that looked like a quiet forest clearing, with some gentle animals crossing a stream and resting under a big old oak tree. It took her many nights, but her quilt ended up just the way she had imagined it.

When Lily showed the quilt to her friends, they all wanted to hear the story behind the animals.

"Once upon a time," Lily began, leaning back on her chair, "There was this wild boowlike thing that liked to swim. It would drift across the creek, just swimming along. One day it got scared, and wandered off into the forest."
